title: Branched Workspace Workflow — Feature Development Example
description: End-to-end example showing how a Contoso developer uses Branched Workspaces to add a 'Customer Churn Prediction' column to the data warehouse and update the analytics report, from branch creation to production deployment.
scenario: Add customer churn prediction score to dim_customer and surface it in the Customer Insights report.

steps.title: Create Git branch
steps.action: Create a Git branch for code changes

details.description: This branch will contain SQL schema changes, notebook updates, and semantic model definition updates.

steps.title: Create workspace branches
steps.action: When the PR is opened, GitHub Actions triggers workspace branch creation via the Fabric REST API.

api_calls.description: Branch the data-warehouse workspace (full isolation — schema changes need isolated data)

body.description: Auto-created for PR #42: Add customer churn prediction score

api_calls.description: Branch the analytics workspace (shared isolation — only report/model definitions)

body.description: Auto-created for PR #42: Add customer churn prediction score

details.note: Full-isolation branch takes 3–5 minutes to provision because it snapshots table data. Shared branch is ready in seconds.

steps.title: Develop schema changes
steps.action: Modify the warehouse schema in the branched workspace to add the churn score column.

changes.change: ALTER TABLE dims.dim_customer ADD churn_risk_score DECIMAL(5,4);
changes.description: Add a new column to store the ML model's churn prediction score (0.0000 to 1.0000).

changes.change: Add churn_risk_score to the customer 360 view with CASE-based risk tier labeling.
changes.description: Expose the score via the view that the semantic model consumes.

details.testing: Run the ALTER TABLE in the branched warehouse (isolated from main). Verify the column exists and accepts data.
details.git_commit: git add . && git commit -m 'feat(warehouse): add churn_risk_score to dim_customer'

steps.title: Update semantic model
steps.action: Add the churn score column and a calculated measure to the contoso_sales semantic model in the branched analytics workspace.

changes.change: Add 'churn_risk_score' column to the dim_customer table definition and create a 'High Risk Customer Count' measure.

details.testing: In the branched analytics workspace, refresh the semantic model. Verify the new column and measure appear in the field list.
details.git_commit: git add . && git commit -m 'feat(semantic-model): add churn risk score and high-risk measure'

steps.title: Update report
steps.action: Add a churn risk visual to the Customer Insights report in the branched analytics workspace.

changes.change: Add a new report page 'Churn Risk' with: (1) Card showing High Risk Customer Count, (2) Scatter plot of lifetime_value vs churn_risk_score, (3) Table of top 50 at-risk customers

details.testing: Open the report in the branched workspace. Verify visuals render correctly with the branched data snapshot.

steps.title: Test with isolated data
steps.action: Run the ML inference notebook in the branched data-engineering workspace to populate the churn scores.

details.description: The branched data-warehouse workspace has a snapshot of dim_customer. The developer runs the churn prediction notebook which writes scores to the branched warehouse. This does NOT affect the main workspace's data.
validation_queries: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dims.dim_customer WHERE churn_risk_score IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(churn_risk_score) as avg_score, MIN(churn_risk_score) as min_score, MAX(churn_risk_score) as max_score FROM dims.dim_customer;
SELECT customer_segment, AVG(churn_risk_score) FROM dims.dim_customer GROUP BY customer_segment;

steps.title: Create merge request (workspace)
steps.action: Create a workspace merge request to merge the branched changes back into the main dev workspace.

api_call.description: Compare the branch with main to see what changed

changes.details: Schema change: 1 column added to dims.dim_customer

dataChanges.note: Data changes (churn scores) in the branch are NOT merged — only schema/definition changes are merged. The ML pipeline will re-run in main to populate the scores.

steps.title: Review and approve
steps.action: Review both the Git PR and the workspace merge request.

checks: SQL schema change is backwards-compatible (additive only)
DAX measure is correct and performant
Semantic model .bim file changes are minimal
Unit tests pass in GitHub Actions
Report page looks correct in the comparison view
No unintended changes to other items

steps.title: Merge
steps.action: Merge both the Git PR and the workspace branch.

order: 1. Git PR merged into main (code is source of truth)
2. Workspace branch merged into main dev workspace (applies item changes)

steps.title: Deploy to production via Deployment Pipeline
steps.action: The merged changes in the dev main workspace are promoted through the Deployment Pipeline: Dev → Test → Prod.

actions: Semantic model rebind to test data source

post_actions: Run ML pipeline in prod to populate churn scores

timeline_summary.day_1: Developer creates Git branch + workspace branches auto-created
timeline_summary.day_1_to_3: Development: schema change, semantic model update, report update
timeline_summary.day_3: Testing in isolated branch with data snapshot
timeline_summary.day_4: Code review (Git PR) + workspace review (merge request)
